documents.path: HR/Performance Management/Performance Evaluation/2011 Compensation Analysis.docx
documents.folder: HR/Performance Management/Performance Evaluation

chunks.text: Introduction and Scope of Review Partners of the Americas’ current compensation structure was analyzed to determine 1) if salaries are comparable to similar organizations in the metropolitan area; 2) if job titles are appropriate with job functions/comparable positions and 3) if positions are classified correctly according to Fair Labor Standards guidelines. Methodology FosterThomas reviewed Partner’s current job descriptions, conducted face-to-face interviews with Partners’ staff and reviewed salary surveys from 1) The Human Resources Association of the National Capital Area (HRA-NCA), 2) InsideNGO, Professionals for NonProfits (PNP) DC Salary Survey 2010 and 3) the Economic Research Institute (ERI). Job analysis was conducted to determine what activities and responsibilities are included in the job, their relative importance in comparison with other jobs, the personal qualifications necessary for performance of the jobs and the conditions under which the work is performed. Pay structures were set by collecting, analyzing and matching job salary survey data with rates paid in the external market. Findings/Recommendations Based on a comparison of Partners to comparable non-profit organizations and International NGO’s in the Washington, DC metro area, Partners’ current salary structure is in line with comparable organizations. However, FosterThomas noted the following differences between Partners and comparable non-profit organizations: Program Officer: Partners is top heavy at the Program Officer level. It is recommended that Partners create additional levels within the Program Officer category, such as Program Officer I and Program Officer II (Senior Program Officer). Program Officer II would be a higher level than I with years of experience as the most likely distinction. Program Officer I would have 2 – 3 years of work experience while Program Officer II would have 3 – 5 years of work experience. Deputy Director: This is not a widely used title within other

comparable organizations. It is recommended that Partners use the title Assistant Director for this position as the next step from the Program Officer II position. Program Director: As with the Program Officer position, it is recommended that Partners create an additional step within the Program Director category. A Program Director II (Senior Program Director) would be distinguished by additional years of experience and a higher level of responsibility. This creates further opportunity for professional development within the organization. Salary Grades Under the current structure, the existing positions within Partners would fall into the following grades: Grade A Program Assistant Grade B Program Officer I Program Officer II (Senior Program Officer) Communications Officer Grade C Finance Officer Administration Officer Grade D Assistant Director Grade E Program Director I Program Director II (Senior Program Director) Grade F Controller Assistant Vice President Vice President Salary Guidelines FosterThomas recommends the following scale for Partners to use as a guideline for starting salaries. Performance, education, level of responsibility, and availability of funds, among other factors, must also be considered when determining grade level and salary. Relevant experience refers to time spent performing the essential functions of that position or a comparable position. The experience minimums suggested at each level refer only to years of relevant experience within that grade. The last column suggests likely years of combined, or total, work experience. Grade Minimum Level I Level II Combined Grade A $22,500 $24,750 $30,938 (Min. 1 year in A) 0 – 2 years Grade B $32,760 $36,036 $45,045 (Min. 2 – 3 yrs. in B) 2 – 5 years Grade C $41,940 $46,134 $57,668 (Min. 2 – 3 yrs. in C) 2 – 5 years Grade D $51,120 $56,232 $70,290 (Min. 3 – 5 yrs. in D) 5 years + Grade E $61,320 $67,452 $84,315 8 years + Grade F $71,540 $78,694 $98, 368 10 years + *This scale can be adjusted to

factor in cost-of-living increases on an annual basis. Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) Classification Exempt/Non-Exempt Definitions A non-exempt employee is one who is covered by the provisions of the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) and is entitled to receive overtime (1 ½ times regular rate) for all hours worked beyond 40 in a workweek. Exempt employees are those employees in the organization to whom the overtime requirements of the law do not apply. The FLSA exempts categories of “white-collar” jobs from minimum wage and overtime requirements if they meet certain tests regarding job duties and responsibilities and are paid a certain minimum salary. This includes Executive, Administrative, Professional, Computer and Outside Sales employees. Exempt employees generally must be paid at least $455 per week on a salary basis. FosterThomas recommends the following classifications: Grade A Program Assistant FLSA Non-exempt Grade B Program Officer I FLSA Exempt Grade B Program Officer II FLSA Exempt Grade C Finance Officer FLSA Exempt Grade C Administration Officer FLSA Exempt Grade D Assistant Director FLSA Exempt Grade E Program Director I FLSA Exempt Grade E Program Director II FLSA Exempt Grade F Controller FLSA Exempt Grade F Assistant Vice President FLSA Exempt Grade F Vice President FLSA Exempt Job Descriptions It is recommended that Partners audit their job descriptions every few years by monitoring the employee’s duties, observing the employee performing his/her job, interviewing the employee, managers and others who interact with the employee and comparing the job to others in the same department and job level.

chunks.text:
MEMO: Performance Improvement 
TO: [EMPLOYEE NAME]
FROM: [SUPERVISOR NAME]
DATE: [DATE]
SUBJECT: Performance Improvement Plan
________________________________________
[REASON FOR PIP]
During the past [specify dates if available], it has become increasingly evident to your [team leader or supervisor] that you have not been performing your assigned work in accordance with what is expected of [job title]. On [dates of all counseling and verbal warning sessions], you were counseled about this unacceptable performance. To date, there has not been any significant improvement. Partners of the Americas values you as an employee, and it is your leadership team's intent to make you fully aware of this situation and to assist you in improving your work performance. However, it is important that you realize the responsibility to improve is yours alone.
You are being placed on a written improvement plan. For the next [30, 60, or 90] days, [DATE] to [DATE], your work will be closely monitored by your leadership team. You must demonstrate immediate improvement in the following areas:
[BODY OF PLAN]
[Identify areas of improvement. Be specific about what needs to be improved. If possible, list objectives with specific deadlines on a separate sheet and attach it to the plan.]
TIPS FOR WRITING OBJECTIVES 
Define specific performance objectives that need to be met. Objectives should be specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time oriented. The recommended format for writing an objective is:
TO: (Action to be taken)
SO THAT: (Desired results)
BY: (MM/DD/YY)
STANDARD: (measurement of results)
[MONITORING THE PLAN]
[Name the person(s) monitoring the plan] will review your progress on each of the above items requiring improvement every [day or week and time or review]. 
[OR ELSE]
Improvement must occur immediately and must be maintained. If any portion of this improvement plan is violated at any time during the specified timeframe, disciplinary action to include separation from the company may occur. A decrease in performance after successfully completing the improvement plan may result in being dismissed from Partners of the Americas without the issuance of another warning or improvement plan.
As always, the Open Door Policy is available for you to discuss any concerns.
Your signature acknowledges this discussion. It does not indicate agreement or disagreement with this plan.

chunks.text:
Alarm Instructions
Our security alarm system is provided and monitored by Kastle Security Systems.
The main security alarm is located in Suite 700 and the reader is adjacent to the reception desk. The alarm will automatically arm and disarm- It will be armed Monday to Friday from 8pm to 8am, and disarmed from 8:01am to 7:59pm Monday to Friday. The security alarm will be armed all day Saturday & Sunday.
In addition, Partners Suites located on the 4th Floor have a key reader located outside of the main door entry. Although these readers do not have a sensor tied to a siren, the activity is still monitored by Kastle. 
Furthermore, ONLY Partners staff has the ability to arm and disarm the security alarm by swiping their key fob on the reader during nonbusiness hours. If the alarm is tripped Kastle will follow the set protocol- Kastle will notify the primary contact (Kristian Vasquez) with a phone call and a follow up email to inquire how to proceed. Kastle will follow the instructions given by the Primary Contact. Should the Primary Contact not be available, Kastle will then contact the secondary and tertiary point of contacts (F&A Team Members).
Dept./ Program Directors have been given 2 extra key fobs so that at their discretion, they may assign it to their intern or consultant. The Director will be responsible for managing the two extra key fobs. For example, the Director will make sure to give and receive the key fob back from the intern or consultant upon start and ending of employment. Additional keys will not be given, each program may decide to purchase additional key fobs ( $40 each ) if needed. If Directors are informed that either of their extra key fobs is misplaced, lost, or stolen you will need to notify F&A. At which time, a replacement key fob will be ordered and the cost of $40 each will be charged to the program/dept.
Please make an important note of the following- key fobs assigned to interns and consultants will have restricted access. They will only have access to our suites during business hours from 8am to 5:30pm Monday to Friday - no weekend access. NO EXCEPTIONS.
Finally, to arm or disarm follow these steps:
To Arm- Swipe your key fob on the reader pad located adjacent to the reception desk. The light will go from green to red. You will have 30 seconds to clear the room.
To Disarm- Swipe your key fob on the reader pad located adjacent to the reception desk. The light will go from red to green.

documents.filename: mindSHIFT Spam Filtering - End User FAQ.docx
documents.folder: HR/Recruitment Process Employees/13) POA Policies & New Hire Forms/Info (Includes Benefits Info & Other Forms)/IT Orientation Materials

chunks.text:
mindSHIFT Spam Filtering FAQ
General Info1
Logging on to McAfee1
Forgot password?2
Changing your password2-3
How to change settings Spam settings3-4
What about messages caught in the filter?5
More Information?5

General Info 
mindSHIFT has partnered with McAfee to deliver your Spam filtering system. Incoming mail is routed directly to McAfee, thus stopping it before it reaches the server. The significance to you is that filtered spam will reside in a secondary location. 
The Email Defense Service (EDS) enables you to manage your personal message quarantine system.  EDS quarantines emails that are suspected to be spam in a safe location (the Control Console).  EDS provides the following tools to manage your email quarantine:
•HTML Spam Quarantine Reports (SQR), which are emailed to you on a regular basis. 
•The Control Console, which you log into either by using a Web browser or clicking a link in the Spam Quarantine Report.
Each email account can be individually tweaked for sensitivity to various types of spam. The ideal way to use these features is outlined below. 
Logging on to Spam Filtering Console
Your account will be set up the weekend of deployment. To access your account, go to: https://portal.mxlogic.com.  You will see the following sign in page:  
Click on “Forgot your password or need to create a password.”  Enter your email address and click “Next.”  You will receive an email from McAfee that includes a link to change your password.  Click on the link and you will be redirected to the Control Console.  From here you will enter your desired password.  Confirm password, then click “Sign In."
Forgot password?
If you forgot your password you can retrieve it on your own. Merely follow the steps as above.
 Changing your password
Note: This capability might not be available if your company’s login authentication process synchronizes your Spam filtering console password with your local network password. In addition, your service might be administered so that the ability for users to change passwords is not available.
To change your password, perform the following steps:
1  Log into the Control Console or access the Control Console by clicking a link in a Spam    Quarantine Report or Spam Quarantine Summary.
       2  Click Setup.
                   The Preferences screen is displayed.
3  Next to the Password field, click Change.
The Password and Confirm Password fields are displayed. Type the new password in the fields. See Reset Your Password from the Sign in Page for information on the proper password format.
    4   For the Security Question field, click Change if you need to change the security question   or answer. You must answer this question if you forget your password and need to reset it.
The Security Question and Security Answer fields are displayed. Select a question from the Security Question drop-down menu. Type the answer to the question in the Security Answer field.
5 Click Save.
Your password is set. You must use this password the next time you log in to the Control Console.
Manage Email Filters and Spam Reporting
Note: The following features are optional and may be unavailable if your EDS Administrator disabled them.
Note: These settings affect all your associated email addresses, including your primary address and all alias email addresses.
To manage your email filters and spam reporting, perform the following steps:
1Click Setup > Preferences.
2Complete the following fields in the Email Defense Settings section.

Spam Filtering
Click the Enable checkbox to turn on spam filtering. Uncheck the Enable box to turn spam filtering off.
The following occurs if you uncheck the Enable checkbox:
•All messages in your spam quarantine are released to your primary email address.
•The fields relating to spam filtering change to be unavailable (grayed).
•Your email is not filtered for spam thereafter.
 Spam Actions, Use Defaults
Select this checkbox if emails determined to have a medium or high likelihood of being spam should be filtered according to the spam policies defined for your policy. The following two fields on the screen are disabled when this checkbox is selected.
If you uncheck the checkbox, you define the actions for spam in the fields that follow.
Medium Spam Action 
From the dropdown menu, select the action applied to emails with a medium likelihood of being spam, based on a series of algorithms EDS uses to determine the likelihood of spam.
Prepend “[SPAM]” to message subject – The phrase “[SPAM]” is added to the subject line of the email and the email is sent to you.
Quarantine the message – The email is added to the spam quarantine area and is not sent to you. 
Deny Delivery – The email is blocked automatically. Depending on the sending system’s configuration, the email sender may or may not be notified.
Do Nothing – The email is sent to you with no actions taken.
High Spam Action field
From the dropdown menu, select the action applied to emails with a high likelihood of being spam.
Prepend “[SPAM]” to message subject – The phrase “[SPAM]” is added to the subject line of the email and the email is sent to you.
Quarantine the message – The email is added to the spam quarantine area and is not sent to you. 
Deny Delivery – The email is blocked automatically. Depending on the sending system’s configuration, the email sender may or may not be notified.
Do Nothing – The email is sent to you with no actions taken.
Spam Quarantine Report Frequency 
From the dropdown menu, select how often you will receive Spam Quarantine Reports if you have email in the spam quarantine area.
The value preceded with the word Default is the value in your policy. 
This field may be unavailable if disabled for your policy.
Spam Quarantine Report Content
Designates the content of each Spam Quarantine Report sent to you.
Default – The value matches the default settings in the policy set. If the settings in the policy are changed, the Default value in this field automatically changes to match.
All Quarantined Messages – All emails in your spam quarantine area are listed in the Spam Quarantine Report.
New Items Since Last Report – Only those emails received since the previous Spam Quarantine Report are listed in the Spam Quarantine Report.
Text-only Summary – A text-only email notification is sent to you with a link to your spam quarantine, instead of the Spam Quarantine Report. This option supports users with email applications that do not support HTML content.
This field may be unavailable if disabled for your policy.

3Click Save.
What about messages caught in the filter?
When you logon to your Spam filtering console, you will see the mail that has been filtered. 
You can do three things with this email: 
Ignore it. It will disappear in 7 days. 
Allow it through by selecting it the message and clicking deliver. 
Delete it by selecting it and clicking “remove”
I need more information!
mindSHIFT provides your Spam filtering service, so you have a couple of options: 
Call mindSHIFT Customer Care for help
The full version of this user guide is available upon request

chunks.text:
New Employee Orientation
Per our manual, each new employee must meet with every program or department for a brief orientation. These orientations should be completed within the first two or three weeks after your hire date. You should plan on approximately 30 minutes for each meeting.
It is your responsibility to take the initiative in planning these meetings, as it is a key component of the orientation and integration process. Of course your supervisor and colleagues are here to support you if you need help with anything! 
It is strongly recommended that you send each manager an invite via Outlook as a way to initiate the scheduling process for these meetings.  If they are not available within the first three weeks of your employment, offer to set up a meeting with another member of their team. This must be completed within the first 30 days of your start date. Once you have completed the orientations, please return to the Finance & Administration Office.

chunks.text:
Web Time Sheet Process Through Employee Self-Service
To begin using the Web Time Clock, first log in to Employee Self-Service. To access the 
Paycom Employee Self-Service website go to www.paycom.com Then select 
“Employee.”
Enter your Username, password and the last four digits of your Social Security number. Then select “Log In.” If this is your first time logging in you will need to reset your password.
From the main menu screen, select “Web Time Sheet” from the Time Management tile in the center of the screen or from the Main Menu navigation on the left side of the page.
On the Time Sheet screen, select the pay period you would like to complete the timesheet for. Then select “Add Hours”. 
The screen below is what you will see to add your hours to your timesheet. From here, you must:
Select the Day(s) 
The Department to which you wish to allocate your time to
Pay Code : Regular, Vacation, etc.
Hours
Comments (Optional)
If you are only charging time to an Administrative Unit you will only need to select the Department.
If you are charging time to a Grant then you must select a Department and its corresponding Grant.
When charging vacation, sick, or other leave you don’t have to select a Department or Grant.
When finished, click on the green “Add Hours” button.
After you’ve added your hour(s), you will see them show on your Web Time Sheet. Once you have completed your time sheet please make sure to click on the green “Approve” button.

chunks.text:
HOW TO REQUEST TIME OFF –VACATION OR SICK LEAVE
Log into the Paycom Employee Self Service using your credentials.
From the Employee Self Service home page, select the “Request Time Off” title.
Once the calendar opens, select the corresponding month for which you will be submitting your request for and click “Go”.
Roll your mouse over the corresponding date, and a green “+” sign will appear on the upper right hand corner. Click on the green “+” sign and the time off window will open.
In the Request Time Off window, make sure to fill in the following information:
Hours per day (8)
Type (Vacation or Sick)
Day or Days (You may select a single day or a range of dates.)
Ignore Weekends
Don’t select Start Time
Reason (Optional)
Finally click the green “Add Request” button. 
Your page will reload and take you to your Request Time Off calendar homepage. You will see your requested dates highlighted in yellow. Once you’ve submitted a request an email will be sent to your supervisor. He/She will be required to approve or deny your request. Once they have either approved or denied your request an email confirmation will be sent to you.

Acknowledgment of Receipt
I have received the Employee Manual of Partners of the Americas (“Partners”).  I understand that the purpose of the Manual is to acquaint employees with the policies and procedures of Partners, and to act as a reference for employees.  I understand that the Manual reflects the policies and procedures in effect this date which may be changed by Partners, and that it does not create any express or implied contractual rights.  I further understand that I am employed on an “at-will” basis, meaning that I have a right to terminate my employment at any time for any reason, and Partners reserves the right to do the same.
I understand that I am bound by the provisions of this Manual and that it is my responsibility to read the contents of the Manual.  I agree to promptly read the Manual and to ask my supervisor any questions that I may have about it. 
I understand that Partners of America may, at its discretion, revise any policy or benefit described in the manual, with or without notice, consistent with applicable law.

Employee SignatureDate

chunks.text: Statement of Confidentiality of Corporate Affairs The nature of Partners’ work makes it essential that employees or representatives do not divulge proprietary company information unless expressly authorized by an officer of the company. All inquiries about Partners’ corporate policies or details of our business or work products are to be referred to or reviewed with an officer of the company. Unless authorized by a company officer, employees or representatives may not reveal details of Partners’ clientele and contracts. As required by FAR 52.203-18, FAR 52.203-19, and FAR 3.909, Partners does not require employees, representatives, and contractors to sign internal confidentiality agreements that could prevent or impede the reporting of fraud, waste, or abuse to appropriate authorities. Employees, representatives, and contractors are encouraged to report concerns, suspicions, or allegations of fraud, waste, abuse, or other misconduct to their supervisor, any member of management, HR, or to Foster Thomas at bbulger@fosterthomas.com. Anonymous reporting options are available and will be treated confidentially to the extent permitted by law. Partners prohibits retaliation against any employee or representative who reports or participates in the investigation of suspected fraud, waste, abuse, or any other misconduct in violation of laws, regulations, or company policies. Use of Proprietary and Confidential Information Partners’ employees and its representatives may have access to and become acquainted with proprietary and confidential information such as client lists, rates, financial records, internal structure, internal policies, publications, inventories, computer software and related documentation and similar information, which is owned by Partners and which is regularly used in the operation of the business of the company. I agree not to use such proprietary or confidential information obtained during employment or contractual relationship with Partners as a means

of making private profit. Nondisclosure of Proprietary or Confidential Information I recognize and acknowledge that certain information concerning Partners’ business is a valuable, special and unique asset of the company. I agree not to disclose Partners’ proprietary or confidential information to any person or entity for any reason, except as required in the course of employment or contractual relationship, or required by law or court order; and further agree not to make use of such information for my own benefit or the private benefit of others. Ownership of Property All files, records, documents, drawings, specifications, equipment and similar items relating to the business of Partners, including computer software or software documentation, whether prepared by me or otherwise coming into my access or possession, shall remain the exclusive property of Partners and shall not be reproduced, removed or transmitted from the premises of the company under any circumstances without the prior written consent of the company. Upon termination of employment with Partners, I will return all company property to my supervisor or a company officer. I agree that if any trade secret, formula, device, technology, technical information, process, etc. (“invention”) is made by me during the course of my employment or contractual relationship, alone or jointly with others which is made with Partners’ equipment, supplies, facilities, trade secrets or time, or which relates to the business of Partners, or which results from any work performed by me for Partners, such invention will belong to Partners and Partners will have full authority to use, transfer or license it. I agree that Partners will have the right to keep such invention as a trade secret, if it so chooses. I further agree to assign to Partners my rights in any invention where the company is required to grant those rights to the United States Government. It is further agreed that Partners is not required to designate me as

the inventor or author of any such invention if it is distributed publicly or otherwise. I understand that violation of this policy is cause for immediate dismissal or termination of contractual relationship and/or demand for financial restitution to Partners for damages to the limit allowed by law. I have read and agree to this policy. Print Name Employee SignatureDate

chunks.text:
Code of Conduct
The No code or policy can anticipate every situation that may arise. Accordingly, this Code is intended to serve as a source of guiding principles. Partner of the Americas (Partners) reserves the right to make all decisions on a case-by-case basis.
Introduction Each employee, volunteer, consultant and representative and member of the Partners Network is expected to adhere to a high standard of ethical conduct. The good name of Partners of the Americas depends on the way its network and representatives conduct business and the way the public perceives that conduct. Unethical actions, or the appearance of unethical actions, are not acceptable. 
Confidentiality All persons interacting with or representing the Partners Network should maintain the confidentiality of information entrusted to them by Partners of the Americas and any other confidential information about the organization, its activities, partners, funders, stakeholders or suppliers, that comes to them, from whatever source, except when disclosure is authorized or legally mandated. 
International Activity Partners of the Americas conducts operations and activities around the world. Many of the countries where Partners conducts business have laws and regulations that may be significantly different from those of the US. Persons interacting with or representing Partners in a foreign country are responsible for knowing and complying with these laws. Furthermore, anyone interacting with or representing the Partners Network must also take care to understand and respect the cultures and customs of the countries where Partners operates. 
Anyone working in the international arena should be aware of the US Foreign Corrupt Practices Act. The Act prohibits giving anything of value to officials of foreign governments in order to obtain or retain business. It is prohibited for any representative of Partners of the Americas or its network to engage in bribery, kickbacks, payoffs or other corrupt business practices. It is also Partners of the Americas’ policy to fully comply with all applicable US export, customs and trade control and regulations, licensing/ trademarking/ copyright requirements and other relevant US and international laws. 
Harassment Partners of the Americas intends to provide a pleasant, healthful, comfortable, and professional atmosphere that is free from intimidation, hostility or other offenses which might interfere with the pursuit of our mission. Moreover, Partners does not tolerate discrimination on the basis of sex, race, color, age, religion, disability, national origin, sexual orientation, or any other legally prohibited characteristic. Harassment or discrimination based on sex or any other legally prohibited basis is illegal and is contrary to the policy of Partners. 
Compliance If you know of or suspect a violation of applicable laws, rules or regulations or this Code, you must immediately report that information to any Senior Manager or member of the International Board of Partners of the Americas, Inc. They could also contact Partners confidential hotline at bbulger@fosterthomas.com. The President or Chairman of the Board shall determine, or shall designate appropriate persons to determine, appropriate action in response to violations of this Code. 
Partners of the Americas prohibits any retaliation against anyone who makes a good faith report of a suspected violation or who participates in an investigation. There is to be no interference, coercion, discrimination, reprisal or retaliation against any person for making a valid and truthful complaint or good faith report of a violation. Nothing in this policy is meant to, nor should it be interpreted to, in any way limit your rights under any applicable federal, state, or local laws.
I have read and agree to this policy.
Print Name
SignatureDate

chunks.text: Conflict of Interest Policy The following Conflict of Interest Policy has been adopted by the Partners International Board of Directors. Each board member, officer, employee, volunteer, intern, and independent contractor of Partners must sign this statement annually to reflect the absence of any relationships or financial interests described in the policy or disclose any which do or may exist. For this policy the definition of “personal relationship” is as follows: grandparents, parents, stepparents, in-laws, spouse, spouse’s family, sibling, sibling’s spouses, sibling’s in-laws, children, stepchildren, ex-spouse, aunts, uncles, cousins, nieces, nephews, and other close friends. Partners seeks to avoid any potential conflicts of interest created by close personal relationships between employees and vendors, contractors, beneficiaries, grantees, services providers, or any other organizations/persons working or planning to work with Partners. Individuals are prohibited from providing assistance or services through any Partners program, such as materials, money, or other goods, services, or resources to anyone with whom they have a close personal relationship. Employees are also prohibited from supervising and participating either directly or indirectly in the hiring, promotion, or evaluation of any individual with whom they have a close personal relationship. Contracts or transactions (i) between Partners and one or more of its board members, officers, or employees, or (ii) between Partners and any corporation, partnership, association or other organization in which one or more of its board members, officers or employees are directors or officers, or have a financial interest, shall not be void or voided solely for this reason, or solely because the board member, officer or employee is present at or participates in any meeting which authorizes the contract or transaction, or solely because his, her, or their votes are counted for such purpose, if: The material facts

regarding the relationship or interest are disclosed or are known to Partners and/or the Board of Directors and in good faith authorized the contract or transaction by the affirmative votes of a majority of the disinterested board members or officers, even though the disinterested board members or officers may then become less than a quorum; or The material facts regarding the relationship or interest are disclosed or are known to the Partners and/or Board of Directors and officers voting on the contract or transaction is specifically approved in good faith by a vote; or The contract or transaction is fair to Partners as of the time it is authorized, approved or ratified. Common or interested Board members or officers may be counted in determining a quorum at any meeting which authorizes the contract or transaction, provided, however, that the interested person or persons shall be excluded from the room during the discussion of the contract or transaction in question and during the vote. The policy requires all individuals to immediately notify their supervisor, the HR Department at poahr@partners.net or the Compliance Team at compliance@partners.net in writing of any potential issues that may arise during the performance of their work, which may constitute a conflict of interest. When informed of a close personal relationship in accordance with this policy, supervisor and/or the Compliance Team will recommend steps to eliminate any potential conflict of interest. In the case of a supervisor-subordinate close personal relationship, a reasonable effort will be made to reassign one of the employees to another position. Failure to disclose the existence of a close personal relationship in accordance with this policy may be cause for termination. Any employee who believes this policy is being violated should report the concern to their supervisor, the HR Department at poahr@partners.net or the Compliance Team at compliance@partners.net. I have read and agree to this
policy. Print Name SignatureDate

chunks.text:
Productive Work Environment/Sexual Harassment 
Partners intends to provide for its employees, and non-employees (such as interns, volunteers, consultants and independent contractors), a work environment that is pleasant, healthful, comfortable, and free from intimidation, hostility or other offenses which might interfere with their performance. Harassment of any sort – verbal, physical, environmental – is prohibited by this policy and will not be tolerated. Moreover, Partners will not tolerate harassment by any employee, non-employee or member of the Partners network. 
Partners does not tolerate discrimination on the basis of sex, race, color, creed, age, religion, disability, national origin, or any other legally prohibited characteristic. Harassment based on sex or any other legally prohibited basis is illegal and is contrary to the policy of Partners. 
Partners will not tolerate verbal or physical conduct which harasses, disrupts or interferes with work performance or which creates an intimidating, offensive or hostile work environment. In order to avoid circumstances where harassment becomes severe or pervasive, employees and non-employees should report all incidents of harassment immediately. While all forms of harassment are prohibited, Partners emphasizes that sexual harassment is specifically prohibited. Each employee and non-employee has the responsibility to ensure that the workplace is free of any form of sexual harassment. No Partners employees, non-employees or members of the Partners network shall threaten or insinuate, either explicitly or implicitly, that any person’s refusal to submit to sexual advances will adversely affect the work.
Other sexually harassing conduct in the workplace is also prohibited. Such conduct includes, but is not limited to: a) unwelcome sexual flirtations, touching, advances, or propositions; b) verbal abuse of a sexual nature; c) graphic or sexually suggestive comments; d) sexually degrading words to describe an individual; and e) the display, storage or transmission of sexually suggestive objects or pictures. 
Harassment Complaint Procedure 
If you believe you have been subjected to prohibited harassment or you are aware of any such conduct, the incident must be reported immediately, preferably in writing, to any supervisor or Senior Manager of Partners of the Americas. All complaints will be promptly and thoroughly investigated and each complaint will be handled as confidentially as possible under the circumstances. Partners prohibits retaliation against any person who files a harassment complaint or cooperates with an investigation. Complaints will be investigated impartially and if found to be truthful, Partners will take immediate corrective action. Anyone found to have engaged in harassment will be subject to appropriate disciplinary action up to, and including termination. 
Retaliation for Harassment Complaint 
Partners prohibits any retaliation against anyone who legitimately exercise their rights under this policy or who participate in an investigation. There is to be no interference, coercion, discrimination, reprisal or retaliation against any person for making a valid and truthful complaint or report of a violation. Any employees or non-employees who believe they are being discriminated against as a result of having legitimately filed a complaint or having truthfully participated in an investigation should immediately notify a member of management. Anyone who becomes aware of possible harassment or retaliation must immediately advise Partners of the Americas so it can be investigated in a timely and confidential manner.
This policy applies to all individuals when interacting with members of the Partners staff or network.
I have read and agree to this policy.
SignatureDate
Name (Please Print)

chunks.text: This is to acknowledge that __________________________________________ has received the Anti-Harassment Training provided by FosterThomas on (Insert Training Date Here). The above employee will comply with all requirements set forth in the training in addition to the company’s revised policies, included below. ________________________________________________________________________________ Employee Signature Date Grievance Policy It is the policy of Partners to provide employees with the opportunity to bring work-related problems and concerns to the attention of management and to resolve such problems fairly and promptly. Any employee who feels that s/he has been treated unfairly or inequitably in regards to a condition or action affecting employment should bring the matter to the attention of his/her supervisor, or a member of Senior Management. Whistleblower Policy Employees have the duty to comply with all applicable laws and to assist Partners to ensure legal compliance. Partners encourages its employees to report improper activities in the workplace and will protect employees from retaliation for making any such report in good faith. Employees have the right to report, without suffering retaliation, any activity by the organization or its employees that the employee reasonably believes: Violates any state or federal law; Violates or amounts to noncompliance with a state or federal rule or regulation; Violates fiduciary responsibilities by a corporation to its stakeholders or employees. In addition, employees can refuse to participate in an activity that would result in a violation of state or federal statutes, or a violation or noncompliance with a state or federal rule or regulation. Employees are also protected from retaliation for having exercised any of these rights in any former employment. The whistleblower protection laws do not entitle employees to violate a confidential privilege (such as attorney-client privilege) or improperly disclose trade-secret

information. Whistleblower Complaint Procedure Any employee who suspects a violation of ethics or legal compliance is required to report the situation(s) to his/her supervisor or a member of Senior Management. Alternatively, a confidential hotline at (202) 247-9610 has been established for you to report your concern via voicemail message to an outside party. The voicemail is monitored by an HR Consultant from FosterThomas who will relay any concerns to the Ethics Committee of the Board. If the situation is life-threatening or extremely critical in nature, it needs to be reported to Senior Management as well the hotline for immediate action to be taken. Employees may also report information regarding possible unlawful activity to an appropriate government or law enforcement agency. Protection from Retaliation for Whistleblower Complaint Federal and state laws provide protection from discharge or other discriminatory actions by employers in retaliation for employees’ good faith complaints about safety and health hazards or other violations of law in the workplace and the environment. The whistleblower programs do not preempt existing state statutes and common law claims. All provisions contained in the programs are in addition to protection provided by state laws. Productive Work Environment/Sexual Harassment Partners intends to provide a work environment that is pleasant, healthful, comfortable, and free from intimidation, hostility or other offenses which might interfere with work performance. Harassment of any sort – verbal, physical, environmental – is prohibited by this policy and will not be tolerated. Moreover, Partners will not tolerate harassment by supervisors, employees, volunteers, or other non-employees who conduct business with Partners. Partners does not tolerate discrimination on the basis of sex, race, color, creed, age, religion, disability, national origin, or any other legally prohibited characteristic. Harassment based on sex or any other legally

prohibited basis is illegal and is contrary to the policy of Partners. Partners will not tolerate verbal or physical conduct which harasses, disrupts or interferes with another’s work performance or which creates an intimidating, offensive or hostile work environment. In order to avoid circumstances where harassment becomes severe or pervasive, employees should report all incidents of harassment immediately. While all forms of harassment are prohibited, Partners emphasizes that sexual harassment is specifically prohibited. Each staff member has the responsibility to ensure that the workplace is free of any form of sexual harassment. No supervisor or other employee shall threaten or insinuate, either explicitly or implicitly, that an employee’s refusal to submit to sexual advances will adversely affect the staff member’s employment, wages, advancement, assigned duties, or any other condition of employment or career development. Other sexually harassing conduct in the workplace is also prohibited. Such conduct includes, but is not limited to: Unwelcome sexual flirtations, touching, advances, or propositions; Verbal abuse of a sexual nature; Graphic or sexually suggestive comments; Sexually degrading words to describe an individual; and The display, storage or transmission of sexually suggestive objects or pictures. Harassment Complaint Procedure If you believe you have been subjected to prohibited harassment or you are aware of any such conduct, the incident must be reported immediately. We ask that any person who has witnessed or been the subject of harassment contact our independent HR Consultant, FosterThomas. A confidential hotline at (202) 247-9610 has been set up for you to report your concern via voicemail message. The voicemail is monitored by an HR Consultant from FosterThomas who will review all messages and promptly contact you to discuss your concern and to gain a full understanding of the situation. The HR Consultant will then provide an overview of the

situation to the Ethics Committee of the Board. The Ethics Committee will then review the case and make a determination of whether the case should be referred to the President for further action. If the complainant provides no identifying information in the message, FosterThomas will relay the complaint directly to the Ethics Committee for review. Alternatively, or in addition to the confidential hotline, you may report harassment to your supervisor. If your supervisor is unavailable, or if you do not feel comfortable contacting that individual, you may also contact another supervisor at Partners. Such complaints must be recorded in writing, either by the complainant or by the supervisor to whom it is reported. (An optional Harassment Complaint Form is available in the Partners HR Handbook or by request to the Finance Office.) Any supervisor who becomes aware of possible harassment or retaliation must immediately advise a member of Senior Management (President or CFO) so it can be investigated in a timely and confidential manner. All harassment complaints will be promptly and thoroughly investigated and each complaint will be handled as confidentially as possible under the circumstances. Complaints will be investigated impartially and if found to be truthful, Partners will take immediate corrective action. Anyone found to have engaged in harassment will be subject to appropriate disciplinary action up to, and including, termination of employment. Protection from Retaliation for Harassment Complaint Partners prohibits any retaliation against employees who legitimately exercise their rights under this policy or who participate in an investigation. There is to be no interference, coercion, discrimination, reprisal or retaliation against any employee for making a valid and truthful complaint or report of a violation. Employees who believe they are being discriminated against as a result of having legitimately filed a complaint or having truthfully participated in an
investigation should immediately notify a member of Senior Management or use the confidential hotline.

chunks.text: This is to acknowledge that __________________________________________ has received the Anti-Harassment Training provided by FosterThomas on (Insert Training Date Here). The above employee will comply with all requirements set forth in the training in addition to the company’s revised policies, included below. ________________________________________________________________________________ Employee Signature Date Grievance Policy It is the policy of Partners to provide employees with the opportunity to bring work-related problems and concerns to the attention of management and to resolve such problems fairly and promptly. Any employee who feels that s/he has been treated unfairly or inequitably in regards to a condition or action affecting employment should bring the matter to the attention of his/her supervisor, or a member of Senior Management. Whistleblower Policy Employees have the duty to comply with all applicable laws and to assist Partners to ensure legal compliance. Partners encourages its employees to report improper activities in the workplace and will protect employees from retaliation for making any such report in good faith. Employees have the right to report, without suffering retaliation, any activity by the organization or its employees that the employee reasonably believes: Violates any state or federal law; Violates or amounts to noncompliance with a state or federal rule or regulation; Violates fiduciary responsibilities by a corporation to its stakeholders or employees. In addition, employees can refuse to participate in an activity that would result in a violation of state or federal statutes, or a violation or noncompliance with a state or federal rule or regulation. Employees are also protected from retaliation for having exercised any of these rights in any former employment. The whistleblower protection laws do not entitle employees to violate a confidential privilege (such as attorney-client privilege) or improperly disclose trade-secret

information. Whistleblower Complaint Procedure Any employee who suspects a violation of ethics or legal compliance is required to report the situation(s) to his/her supervisor or a member of Senior Management. Alternatively, a confidential hotline at (202) 247-9610 has been established for you to report your concern via voicemail message to an outside party. The voicemail is monitored by an HR Consultant from FosterThomas who will relay any concerns to the Ethics Committee of the Board. If the situation is life-threatening or extremely critical in nature, it needs to be reported to Senior Management as well the hotline for immediate action to be taken. Employees may also report information regarding possible unlawful activity to an appropriate government or law enforcement agency. Protection from Retaliation for Whistleblower Complaint Federal and state laws provide protection from discharge or other discriminatory actions by employers in retaliation for employees’ good faith complaints about safety and health hazards or other violations of law in the workplace and the environment. The whistleblower programs do not preempt existing state statutes and common law claims. All provisions contained in the programs are in addition to protection provided by state laws. Productive Work Environment/Sexual Harassment Partners intends to provide a work environment that is pleasant, healthful, comfortable, and free from intimidation, hostility or other offenses which might interfere with work performance. Harassment of any sort – verbal, physical, environmental – is prohibited by this policy and will not be tolerated. Moreover, Partners will not tolerate harassment by supervisors, employees, volunteers, or other non-employees who conduct business with Partners. Partners does not tolerate discrimination on the basis of sex, race, color, creed, age, religion, disability, national origin, or any other legally prohibited characteristic. Harassment based on sex or any other legally

prohibited basis is illegal and is contrary to the policy of Partners. Partners will not tolerate verbal or physical conduct which harasses, disrupts or interferes with another’s work performance or which creates an intimidating, offensive or hostile work environment. In order to avoid circumstances where harassment becomes severe or pervasive, employees should report all incidents of harassment immediately. While all forms of harassment are prohibited, Partners emphasizes that sexual harassment is specifically prohibited. Each staff member has the responsibility to ensure that the workplace is free of any form of sexual harassment. No supervisor or other employee shall threaten or insinuate, either explicitly or implicitly, that an employee’s refusal to submit to sexual advances will adversely affect the staff member’s employment, wages, advancement, assigned duties, or any other condition of employment or career development. Other sexually harassing conduct in the workplace is also prohibited. Such conduct includes, but is not limited to: Unwelcome sexual flirtations, touching, advances, or propositions; Verbal abuse of a sexual nature; Graphic or sexually suggestive comments; Sexually degrading words to describe an individual; and The display, storage or transmission of sexually suggestive objects or pictures. Harassment Complaint Procedure If you believe you have been subjected to prohibited harassment or you are aware of any such conduct, the incident must be reported immediately. We ask that any person who has witnessed or been the subject of harassment contact our independent HR Consultant, FosterThomas. A confidential hotline at (202) 247-9610 has been set up for you to report your concern via voicemail message. The voicemail is monitored by an HR Consultant from FosterThomas who will review all messages and promptly contact you to discuss your concern and to gain a full understanding of the situation. The HR Consultant will then provide an overview of the

situation to the Ethics Committee of the Board. The Ethics Committee will then review the case and make a determination of whether the case should be referred to the President for further action. If the complainant provides no identifying information in the message, FosterThomas will relay the complaint directly to the Ethics Committee for review. Alternatively, or in addition to the confidential hotline, you may report harassment to your supervisor. If your supervisor is unavailable, or if you do not feel comfortable contacting that individual, you may also contact another supervisor at Partners. Such complaints must be recorded in writing, either by the complainant or by the supervisor to whom it is reported. (An optional Harassment Complaint Form is available in the Partners HR Handbook or by request to the Finance Office.) Any supervisor who becomes aware of possible harassment or retaliation must immediately advise a member of Senior Management (President or CFO) so it can be investigated in a timely and confidential manner. All harassment complaints will be promptly and thoroughly investigated and each complaint will be handled as confidentially as possible under the circumstances. Complaints will be investigated impartially and if found to be truthful, Partners will take immediate corrective action. Anyone found to have engaged in harassment will be subject to appropriate disciplinary action up to, and including, termination of employment. Protection from Retaliation for Harassment Complaint Partners prohibits any retaliation against employees who legitimately exercise their rights under this policy or who participate in an investigation. There is to be no interference, coercion, discrimination, reprisal or retaliation against any employee for making a valid and truthful complaint or report of a violation. Employees who believe they are being discriminated against as a result of having legitimately filed a complaint or having truthfully participated in an
investigation should immediately notify a member of Senior Management or use the confidential hotline.

Partners of the Americas’ policy prohibits retaliation against any employee for complaining about discrimination or harassment.

chunks.text: Productive Work Environment/Sexual Harassment Partners intends to provide a work environment that is pleasant, healthful, comfortable, and free from intimidation, hostility or other offenses which might interfere with work performance. Harassment of any sort - verbal, physical, visual - will not be tolerated. Partners will not tolerate verbal or physical conduct which harasses, disrupts or interferes with another’s work performance or which creates an intimidating, offensive or hostile work environment. In order to avoid circumstances where harassment becomes severe or pervasive, employees should report all incidences of harassment immediately. While all forms of harassment are prohibited, Partners emphasizes that sexual harassment is specifically prohibited. Each staff member has the responsibility to ensure that the workplace is free of any form of sexual harassment. No supervisor or other employee shall threaten or insinuate, either explicitly or implicitly, that an employee’s refusal to submit to sexual advances will adversely affect the staff member’s employment, wages, advancement, assigned duties, or any other condition of employment or career development. Other sexually harassing conduct in the workplace is also prohibited. Such conduct includes: a) sexual flirtations, inappropriate touching, advances, or propositions; b) verbal abuse of a sexual nature; c) graphic or sexually suggestive comments; d) sexually degrading words to describe an individual; and e) the display, storing or transmitting of sexually suggestive objects or pictures. Partners accepts no liability for harassment of one employee by another employee. The individual who makes unwelcome advances, threatens or in any way harasses another employee is personally liable for such actions and their consequences. Partners will not provide legal, financial or any other assistance to an individual accused of harassment if a legal complaint is filed. Harassment Complaint Procedure If you believe you

have been subjected to prohibited harassment or you are aware of any such conduct, the incident must immediately be reported, to your supervisor. If your supervisor is unavailable or if you do not feel comfortable contacting that individual, you should contact a senior manager of Partners. All complaints will be promptly and thoroughly investigated and each complaint will be handled as confidentially as possible. Partners ensure that employees following this complaint procedure will be protected against illegal retaliation. Complaints will be investigated impartially and if found to be truthful, Partners will take immediate corrective action. Anyone found to have engaged in harassment will be subject to disciplinary action, up to, and including termination of employment. Retaliation for Sexual Harassment Complaint If you experience or witness sexual or other unlawful harassment in the workplace, report it immediately to your supervisor. If your supervisor is unavailable or you believe it would be inappropriate to discuss it with that person, you should immediately report it to any member of management. All allegations of sexual harassment will be quickly and discreetly investigated. To the extent possible, your confidentiality and that of any witnesses and the alleged harasser will be protected against unnecessary disclosure. When the investigation is completed, you will be informed of the outcome of the investigation. Partners does not tolerate any retaliation against employees who legitimately exercise their rights under this policy or who participate in an investigation. There is to be no interference, coercion, discrimination, reprisal or retaliation against any employee for making a valid and truthful complaint or report of a violation. Employees who believe they are being discriminated against as a result of having legitimately filed a complaint or having truthfully participated in an investigation should immediately notify a member of senior management. Any
supervisor who becomes aware of possible harassment or retaliation must immediately advise a member of senior management so it can be investigated in a timely and confidential manner.

chunks.text:
Productive Work Environment/Sexual Harassment 
Partners intends to provide for its employees, and non-employees (such as interns, volunteers, consultants and independent contractors), a work environment that is pleasant, healthful, comfortable, and free from intimidation, hostility or other offenses which might interfere with their performance. Harassment of any sort – verbal, physical, environmental – is prohibited by this policy and will not be tolerated. Moreover, Partners will not tolerate harassment by any employee, non-employee or member of the Partners network. 
Partners does not tolerate discrimination on the basis of sex, race, color, creed, age, religion, disability, national origin, or any other legally prohibited characteristic. Harassment based on sex or any other legally prohibited basis is illegal and is contrary to the policy of Partners. 
Partners will not tolerate verbal or physical conduct which harasses, disrupts or interferes with work performance or which creates an intimidating, offensive or hostile work environment. In order to avoid circumstances where harassment becomes severe or pervasive, employees and non-employees should report all incidents of harassment immediately. While all forms of harassment are prohibited, Partners emphasizes that sexual harassment is specifically prohibited. Each employee and non-employee has the responsibility to ensure that the workplace is free of any form of sexual harassment. No Partners employees, non-employees or members of the Partners network shall threaten or insinuate, either explicitly or implicitly, that any person’s refusal to submit to sexual advances will adversely affect the work.
Other sexually harassing conduct in the workplace is also prohibited. Such conduct includes, but is not limited to: a) unwelcome sexual flirtations, touching, advances, or propositions; b) verbal abuse of a sexual nature; c) graphic or sexually suggestive comments; d) sexually degrading words to describe an individual; and e) the display, storage or transmission of sexually suggestive objects or pictures. 
Harassment Complaint Procedure 
If you believe you have been subjected to prohibited harassment or you are aware of any such conduct, the incident must be reported immediately, preferably in writing, to any supervisor or Senior Manager of Partners of the Americas. All complaints will be promptly and thoroughly investigated and each complaint will be handled as confidentially as possible under the circumstances. Partners prohibits retaliation against any person who files a harassment complaint or cooperates with an investigation. Complaints will be investigated impartially and if found to be truthful, Partners will take immediate corrective action. Anyone found to have engaged in harassment will be subject to appropriate disciplinary action up to, and including termination. 
Retaliation for Harassment Complaint 
Partners prohibits any retaliation against anyone who legitimately exercise their rights under this policy or who participate in an investigation. There is to be no interference, coercion, discrimination, reprisal or retaliation against any person for making a valid and truthful complaint or report of a violation. Any employees or non-employees who believe they are being discriminated against as a result of having legitimately filed a complaint or having truthfully participated in an investigation should immediately notify a member of management. Anyone who becomes aware of possible harassment or retaliation must immediately advise Partners of the Americas so it can be investigated in a timely and confidential manner.
This policy applies to all individuals when interacting with members of the Partners staff or network.
I have read and agree to this policy.
SignatureDate
Name (Please Print)

chunks.text:
[DATE]
[CANDIDATE NAME]
[ADDRESS]
Dear [CANDIDATE Name]
Partners of the Americas is pleased to offer you the full-time, exempt position of (POSITION NAME), for our (UNIT NAME), beginning (DATE), in our office at 1424 K Street NW, Suite 700, Washington, DC. You will be reporting to (SUPERVISOR NAME, TITLE UNIT).
This letter will serve to outline the offer:
Your responsibilities are those outlined in the Position Description and as described to you during your interviews.
The annual gross salary for this position is (SALARY AMOUNT) based on a 40 hour worked per week. Your first paycheck will be issued on (DATE), corresponding to the pay period of (DATE RANGE)
You are eligible for Partners’ benefits package (described in the attached benefits summary.  
Your performance will be evaluated after a 90 day provisional period.
Your employment and compensation with Partners of the Americas are “at will” in that they can be terminated with or without cause, and with or without notice, at any time, at the option of either Partners of the Americas or yourself, except as otherwise provided by law.  No manager or representative of Partners of the Americas, other than the President and CEO, has authority to enter into any agreement for employment for any specified period or to make any agreement or contract to the foregoing, and any promises to the contrary may only be relied upon by you if they are in writing and signed by the President of Partners of the Americas.
If you accept this offer as outlined above, please sign below and return this letter to Paola Sarmiento at psarmiento@partners.net.  We are confident your employment with Partners of the Americas will prove mutually beneficial, and we look forward to working with you.
Sincerely,Accepted by:
_____________________                             _______________________
John McPhail[CANDIDATE NAME]                               
President & CEO

chunks.text: Employee Recruitment Process: Responsibility Action Supervisor/ Hiring Manager Complete Recruitment Training with HR Identify a need for a new hire. Schedule Recruitment strategy meeting with HR Officer Recruitment Strategy Meeting Outline POA Objectives for meeting: - Provide salary range based on matrix - Walk through on completing a job requisition form. - Determine the structure of the interview process for candidates and who will be involved in interview panels. - Determine a strategy on advertising the position and to which platforms the job will be posted. - Determine onboarding standard training -Schedule recurring weekly recruitment check-in meetings. Prepares position description by either creating a role-specific job description/scope of work or checking with the HR Officer for sample position descriptions that can be used to further customize for the role. Supervisor sends completed PD to HR Officer. Position Description Template HR Officer Initiate Job Requisition Form in PandaDoc that includes compensation grade and position on matrix, source of funding for position. For Home Office Job Requisition is signed by Supervisor, Unit VP/Director, HO HR, and President & CEO to authorize the advertising and publication of the vacancy. For Field Office Job Requisition is signed by Supervisor, Project Director/Chief of Party, FO HR, and HO Senior Director to authorize the advertising and publication of the vacancy. A copy of the position description should be attached. Collect all CVs received from emails, job boards, and websites applying for the position and save in SharePoint folders shared with Hiring manager. Organize applicant CVs based on job requirements/experience/qualifications in folders rated A, B, or C Create initial pre-screen questions based on the following template that covers cultural fit, salary expectation, start date. POA pre-screening questions template Post the publication on the POA website, and any job boards approved by the recruitment

lead. Web Job Post Template Will include the new vacancy in internal announcements to allow current members of the organization exposure to and a chance to apply. Will send announcement to global staff. POA Internal announcement template Perform initial HR screen of candidates indicated by Hiring Manager. Coordinate with Recruitment Lead/Hiring Manager on scheduling technical interviews. Collaborate with supervisor/hiring manager on creating interview questions and formation of panels/committees for interviews. Use POA standard interview questions as the base. Input questions into Microsoft forms and share the link to the questions to all interviewers Schedule a debrief meeting after all interviews have been conducted. Recruitment Lead/ Hiring Manager Review candidates within A, B, or C folders and determine which would be worth an initial HR screen. Hiring Manager/ Selection Committee Interview candidates. Select 2-3 finalists distinguished as 1st, 2nd, or 3rd choice finalists for interview with Unit ELT for HO and HO Senior Director for FO. Attend debrief meeting with HR to select / determine finalist. May conduct skills assessment for candidate. Hiring exercises HR Officer Coordinate finalist interviews. Prepare salary analysis, conduct internal equity check and forward to President/CEO for Home office and Project Director and Sr. Director for Field Office hires Perform reference checks on 1st choice finalist approved by President following interview using the following template: Candidate Reference Check template Hiring Manager/ Selection Committee Complete the Employee Selection Memo and obtain all signatures in PandaDoc. Employee Selection Memo HR Officer Contact indicated finalist following completed reference checks to extend verbal offer and determine start date. Send an official offer letter to be signed by CEO and prospective employee. POA Employment Offer Letter Template Conduct pre-hire background check / Visual ID check on selected new hire once signed

offer letter is received. Background Check Authorization Form Send new hire email to Supervisor, Finance & Accounting and President/CEO informing of new hire, title, location. New Hire Notice Email Template Send pre-boarding new hire forms to include appropriate federal and local tax withholding forms, required employee HRIS data, Form I-9 verification, benefits information, and enrollment to be completed by new employee before first day of service. New Hire Forms Send POA policies packet (the PandaDoc template incorporates all): Code of Conduct Conflict of Interest Harassment Policy Statement of Confidentiality Non-Discrimination Video Release form PSEA Form Child Safeguarding Form Partners Employee Manual Hiring Manager/ Recruitment Lead Complete an FSI request and send to Administration Officer for establishing new hire’s POA email, network access, and order of a new laptop/workstation. FSI request document Email Director of Compliance for new PandaDoc or Replicon account information for field staff. Determine initial trainings, briefings and introductory team/unit specific trainings and coordinate with HR Officer on scheduling these during the first 30 days of new hire’s employment. Onboarding Training Schedule HR Officer Send other finalists that were not selected a rejection email following the template below: Rejection email template.docx

Populate a calendar that serves as the onboarding schedule for both previously determined orientation trainings/briefings by supervisor and send to new hire for their reference.
Ensure completion of administrative items along with signing all necessary documents and setting up for medical insurance on the first day of service.

chunks.text: Nombre del trabajador. Cédula no. [insertar] de [ciudad]. En [ciudad], el [fecha], en las oficinas de PARTNERS OF THE AMERCIAS ubicadas en [dirección], // A través de plataforma electrónica corporativa de Teams el [fecha], se reunieron a partir de las [hora] las siguientes personas: [nombre del trabajador], identificado con cédula de ciudadanía no. [insertar] de [ciudad], en su calidad de trabajador, quien ha sido citado previamente con el propósito de rendir sus descargos. [nombre], en su calidad de [cargo]. [nombre del supervisor], en su calidad de [cargo] y como supervisor inmediato del trabajador, vía virtual. El trabajador ha sido informado de lo siguiente: Que su participación en esta reunión se realiza en cumplimiento de los procedimientos establecidos en el reglamento interno de trabajo de acuerdo con la normatividad vigente y su asistencia es voluntaria. Que esta reunión está siendo grabada para efectos documentales, de la cual se dejará copia escrita mediante un acta. Que, en garantía del derecho de defensa que tienen los trabajadores de Partners, usted podrá aceptar o no la responsabilidad sobre los hechos investigados. Que, si decide responder a las preguntas, se le solicita hacerlo de manera espontánea, concreta, clara y con la verdad, brindando las explicaciones que considere oportunas. Que, en la diligencia usted podrá solicitar o aportar las pruebas que considere necesarias, en cumplimiento a su derecho de defensa. Que, si usted decide retirarse o terminar anticipadamente la reunión, se dejará constancia de lo manifestado hasta ese punto sin posibilidad de modificar, retirar o agregar información posteriormente y, adicionalmente, entenderemos que los hechos que falten por comprobarse se tendrán por ciertos Que, si se niega a firmar el acta, el empleador recurrirá a dos testigos para que verifiquen la veracidad de la situación. chunks.text: Bogotá D. C., 05 de diciembre de 2023. Señor, [nombre] [email] Ciudad. Asunto: Terminación de su contrato de trabajo. Respetado/a Señor/a [apellido], La presente tiene por objeto comunicarle que PARTNERS OF THE AMERICAS ha tomado la decisión unilateral de dar por terminado su contrato de trabajo, sin justa causa, haciendo efectiva dicha terminación a partir de hoy [fecha], desde el momento mismo en que reciba esta notificación. Lo anterior, de conformidad con lo establecido en el artículo 64 del Código Sustantivo del Trabajo, modificado por el artículo 28 de la ley 789 de 2002, según el cual en todo contrato de trabajo va envuelta la condición resolutoria por incumplimiento de lo pactado, con indemnización de perjuicios a cargo de la parte responsable. De acuerdo con esto, se efectuará el pago de la indemnización correspondiente por despido sin justa causa, equivalente al tiempo de servicio prestado, junto con el pago de las prestaciones sociales que por ley le corresponden. De otra parte, se le informa que el pago de sus cotizaciones en seguridad social y parafiscalidad se encuentra al día. Para efectos de sus documentos de retiro la empresa le remitirá a su correo electrónico, la autorización para la realización del examen médico de retiro, certificación laboral y pagos de aportes al sistema general de seguridad social en salud y pensiones. El pago de su liquidación laboral se le podrá efectuar en la cuenta bancaria que usted tenía registrada para efectos de su nómina, por lo que le agradecemos remitir un correo electrónico en tal sentido, si así lo autoriza. En el evento de no recibir una respuesta antes del próximo 06 de diciembre, se procederá con el correspondiente depósito judicial a órdenes de un juzgado laboral. No obstante, aunque la terminación se hace sin justa causa, en seguimiento de lo planteado en el ANEXO F - PARTNERS OF THE AMERICAS (POA) DISPOSICIONES ESTÁNDAR PARTNERS OF THE AMERICAS en su apartado XV. FINALIZACIÓN Y SUSPENSIÓN DEL CONTRATO, le

informamos que las razones internas de la empresa para tomar esta decisión se fundamentan en los siguientes hechos, que la empresa califica como incumplimiento de sus obligaciones contractuales en el desarrollo del objeto, específicamente la de “[mencionar la obligación del contrato /reglamento interno de trabajo que se incumplió]” y de su deber de leal cooperación con la empresa, consagrado en el Reglamento Interno de Trabajo, de acuerdo con: PRIMERO: La organización identificó que, [mencionar la evidencia del incumplimiento con la que cuenta la organización]: SEGUNDO: [mencionar las medidas previamente tomadas (PIP, llamado de atención escrito, sanción, acuerdos y/o compromisos para la mejora y no reincidencia] Para la organización, las explicaciones preliminares [hecho del incumplimiento] no justifican que [comportamiento o falta incurrida] como parte de sus obligaciones, y de acuerdo con la validación y seguimiento realizado por su supervisor [en caso de PIP estos compromisos fueron incumplidos por su parte. TERCERO: El día [fecha], usted fue citado a una reunión para el cierre de [Plan de mejora al desempeño en curso/Diligencia de Descargos], en la cual se expuso el incumplimiento de sus compromisos y obligaciones contractuales sin justificación alguna por su parte, por lo que fue necesario que la empresa decidiera [dar cierre al plan de mejora de forma insatisfactoria/aplicar una sanción equivalente a la terminación de contrato con justa causa motivada]. CUARTO: Como consecuencia de lo anterior, existen elementos para concluir de manera razonable que, desde la perspectiva de cumplimiento de sus obligaciones, y desde la perspectiva de comunicación y colaboración con la empresa, usted ha faltado gravemente al cumplimiento de sus obligaciones laborales, constituyendo una justa causa de despido, en aplicación de lo señalado en el artículo 62, numeral 6 del Código Sustantivo del Trabajo. Atentamente, Nombre Representante legal Partners of The Americas, INC.
Sucursal Colombia Firma en constancia de recibido, Nombre C.C.

Bogotá D.C., [INSERTAR FECHA] de 2024.
Señora/Señor:
[INSERTAR NOMBRE].
Correo: [INSERTAR]
Celular: [INSERTAR]
Ciudad.  
Referencia: terminación del contrato de trabajo con justa causa. 
Respetada/o Señora/Señor [INSERTAR]:  
Me permito informarle que PARTNERS OF THE AMERICAS  ha tomado la decisión unilateral de dar por terminado su contrato de trabajo con justa causa a partir del [DIA] (00) de [MES] de dos mil veinticuatro (2024). 
Lo anterior debido a que usted incumplió las siguientes obligaciones laborales: 
Incumplimiento del numeral 6 del literal A del artículo 62 del Código Sustantivo de Trabajo. 
“ARTICULO 62. TERMINACION DEL CONTRATO POR JUSTA CAUSA. <Artículo modificado por el artículo 7o. del Decreto 2351 de 1965. El nuevo texto es el siguiente:> Son justas causas para dar por terminado unilateralmente el contrato de trabajo:
A). Parte del {empleador}:
(…)
6. Cualquier violación grave de las obligaciones o prohibiciones especiales que incumben al trabajador de acuerdo con los artículos 58 y 60 del Código Sustantivo del Trabajo, o cualquier falta grave calificada como tal en pactos o convenciones colectivas, fallos arbitrales, contratos individuales o reglamentos. (…)”
Incumplimiento del numeral 1 del artículo 58 del Código Sustantivo de Trabajo.
“ARTICULO 58. OBLIGACIONES ESPECIALES DEL TRABAJADOR. Son obligaciones especiales del trabajador:
1a. Realizar personalmente la labor, en los términos estipulados; observar los preceptos del reglamento y acatar y cumplir las órdenes e instrucciones que de modo particular la impartan el empleador o sus representantes, según el orden jerárquico establecido.”
Esto se evidencia en la diligencia de descargos que se llevó a cabo [DIA] (00) de [MES] de dos mil veinticuatro (2024), en donde se abordó y se manifestó lo siguiente:
 [INSERTAR HECHO QUE MOTIVÓ EL PROCESO DE DESCARGOS]. 
SEGUNDO: durante el proceso de descargos usted manifestó y reconoció lo siguiente:
[INSERTAR]
TERCERO: en la diligencia de descargos previamente referida usted no solicitó ni presentó pruebas tendientes a desvirtuar el incumplimiento de las obligaciones laborales, limitándose a aceptar sus faltas.   
De acuerdo con lo anterior, se encuentra debidamente soportada la ocurrencia de una falta grave por incumplimiento de sus obligaciones laborales y, en consecuencia, PARTNERS OF THE AMERICAS se ve en la necesidad de terminar su contrato de trabajo con justa causa, sin perjuicio de que esté facultada para promover las acciones judiciales que considere necesarias para salvaguardar sus derechos.
A su correo electrónico y/o dirección física reportada será enviada la autorización para el examen médico de retiro, la certificación laboral y los pagos de aportes al sistema general de seguridad social en salud y pensiones de los últimos tres (3) meses. 
El pago de su liquidación laboral se efectuará en la cuenta bancaria que usted tenía registrada para efectos de su nómina.
Atentamente, 
____________________
NOMBRE. 
Representante Legal
Partners of the Americas Inc, sucursal Colombia.
